sections.title: Pattern Types
items: Repeating Pattern: A constant value is added throughout. Example: 1, 3, 5, 7, 9, … (+2 each time)
Increasing Pattern: The difference between consecutive numbers itself increases. Example: 2, 4, 7, 11, 16, … (differences: +2, +3, +4, +5, …)
Decreasing Pattern: The difference between consecutive numbers decreases. Example: 42, 38, 32, 24, 14, … (differences: −4, −6, −8, −10, …)
Alternating Pattern: Two or more patterns alternate. Example: 2, 4, 7, 9, 14, 16, … (alternating ×2 and +2)
Letter Pattern: A constant number of letters is skipped. Example: A, D, G, J, M, … (skip 2 letters each time)
Alphanumeric Pattern: Terms combine letters and numbers. Example: A2C, D4F, G6I (letters: skip 1, numbers: +2)
